query: ok i've got this mechanical drawing as a png (it's at tasks/widget/drawing.png) and i need to get all the dimensions and hole callouts out of it into some structured format my modeller can use — can you read it for me
here's a scanned engineering print of a bracket, third-angle projection, dims in inches. pull out every dimension, the GD&T frames, and the bore diameters into JSON for me, in mm
what are the dimensions in this print? i can see a front and top view but i'm not sure which number is the bolt spacing vs the overall width
turn this 2D drawing into a spec I can build from — I don't want to model it yet, just get the views, dims, and features written down faithfully
i have drawing.png in the trial_lbracket task folder, extract the geometry spec from it. units, views present, dims, the 4x and 2x hole groups with diameters and whether they're cut or boss
read the engineering drawing image and tell me the structured dimensions — careful with the inch vs mm thing, the title block says inches
this is a dense PMI drawing with a bunch of feature control frames. i need the position tolerances and datum references extracted along with the linear dims, into the standard schema
got a photo of a part drawing from the shop floor, can you get the hole pattern and the overall envelope out of it as data
model this part in build123d from the spec I just wrote: a 80x50x8 plate with four M5 holes and a central slot. give me the candidate.py
grade my STEP file against the bearing_608 reference and tell me the composite score and per-layer breakdown
the volume on my reconstructed part keeps coming out too high, what's wrong with my build123d code
write me a python script to batch-convert a folder of STEP files to STL using build123d
i have a written spec.md for the motor_mount, reconstruct it as a build123d program and grade it on the spec track
can you read this PDF invoice and pull out the line items and totals into a spreadsheet
extract the text from this scanned contract PDF and summarize the key clauses
generate a 3D model of a dragon for my game, something stylized and organic
what does the reward.py topology layer actually measure and why does my part cap at 0.88
open this drawing.png and just tell me if it looks like a bracket or a flange, i don't need the dimensions
